identity.short_name: Mixed LTE count with prime-cube second digits and quadratic+quartic coupling

problem_spec.summary: Two-prime CRT counting problem where mixed LTE constraints force exact depth-2 valuation at $p$ (difference) and $q$ (sum). Prime-cube "second digits" are defined at $p^3$ and $q^3$, and two interacting couplings (Legendre sign and fourth-power membership) force a 3-category match under CRT.
givens: Parity gate: $\operatorname{lcm}(d_p,d_q)/d_q$ is odd (equivalently $v_2(d_p)\le v_2(d_q)$), so $n/d_q$ is odd.
ask: Let $S\subset(\mathbb{Z}/M\mathbb{Z})^\times$ be the set of classes $x\pmod M$ with $\operatorname{ord}_p(x)=d_p$, $\operatorname{ord}_q(x)=2d_q$, $v_p(x^n-1)=A$, $v_q(x^n+1)=B$, and with prime-cube second digits defined by $x^{d_p}\equiv 1+p^2a\pmod{p^3}$ and $x^{d_q}\equiv -1+q^2b\pmod{q^3}$ satisfying both $\left(\frac{a}{p}\right)=\left(\frac{b}{q}\right)$ and "$a$ is a fourth power mod $p$ iff $b$ is a fourth power mod $q$". Determine $|S|$ and output the unique $N$ with $0\le N<1000$ and $N\equiv |S|\pmod{1000}$.

parameter(s).name: p
parameter(s).purpose: prime for the difference-of-powers LTE branch and quartic-residue digit classification

parameter(s).name: q
parameter(s).purpose: prime for the sum-of-powers LTE branch and quartic-residue digit classification

parameter(s).name: d_p
parameter(s).purpose: prescribed order modulo $p$ for the difference LTE reduction at $p$

parameter(s).name: d_q
parameter(s).purpose: half-order modulo $q$ (forces $x^{d_q}\equiv -1\pmod q$) for the sum LTE reduction at $q$

parameter(s).name: n
parameter(s).purpose: structured exponent that makes the LTE reductions land at exact depth $2$ for the prime-cube digit regime

parameter(s).name: M
parameter(s).purpose: modulus enabling independent prime-power analysis and CRT recombination

invariant(s).name: order2_bridge_to_negative_one
invariant(s).statement: If $\operatorname{ord}_q(x)=2d_q$ then $x^{d_q}$ has order $2$ modulo $q$, hence $x^{d_q}\equiv -1\pmod q$ and $q\mid(x^{d_q}+1)$.

invariant(s).name: parity_gate_for_sum_LTE
invariant(s).statement: $n/d_q$ is odd (equivalently $\operatorname{lcm}(d_p,d_q)/d_q$ is odd), enabling the odd-exponent LTE formula for $v_q(x^n+1)$.

invariant(s).name: LTE_depth_2_reduction
invariant(s).statement: Under the order constraints and parity gate, $v_p(x^n-1)=v_p(x^{d_p}-1)+v_p(n/d_p)$ and $v_q(x^n+1)=v_q(x^{d_q}+1)+v_q(n/d_q)$; choosing $n$ with $v_p(n/d_p)=A-2$ and $v_q(n/d_q)=B-2$ makes the valuation targets equivalent to $v_p(x^{d_p}-1)=2$ and $v_q(x^{d_q}+1)=2$.

invariant(s).name: prime_cube_second_digit
invariant(s).statement: If $v_p(x^{d_p}-1)=2$ then there is a unique $a\in(\mathbb{Z}/p\mathbb{Z})^\times$ with $x^{d_p}\equiv 1+p^2a\pmod{p^3}$, and if $v_q(x^{d_q}+1)=2$ then there is a unique $b\in(\mathbb{Z}/q\mathbb{Z})^\times$ with $x^{d_q}\equiv -1+q^2b\pmod{q^3}$.

invariant(s).name: hensel_unique_prime_square_lift
invariant(s).statement: For odd prime $r$ with $r\nmid m$, any solution of $y^m\equiv\pm 1\pmod r$ lifts uniquely to a solution modulo $r^2$ (simple-root Hensel regime).

invariant(s).name: affine_second_digit_bijection_at_prime_cube
invariant(s).statement: Fix $r$, $m$ with $r\nmid m$ and a lift $y_1$ with $y_1^m\equiv\pm 1\pmod{r^2}$. The lifts $y_1+r^2t$ modulo $r^3$ make the digit $(y^m\mp 1)/r^2\bmod r$ an affine bijection of $t\in\mathbb{Z}/r\mathbb{Z}$; exactly one lift has digit $0$ (depth $\ge 3$), the others give all nonzero digits exactly once.
